observations.recommendation: We recommended management to stop the improper charging of expenses, prepare the necessary adjusting entries for proper presentation in financial statements and cease incurring any expenditure without appropriation. Also, we recommended management to submit justification on why the RATA charged against the appropriation for telephone expenses should not be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction:
None because the budget for CY 2021 was already approved.

Notice of disallowances issued for the payment of transportation allowance were already settled.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to prepare and submit to the office of the auditor the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumptions for review as required by COA Circular No. 77-61 which provides the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to comply with COA Circular No. 77-61 which provides the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles and Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445. All expenses regarding any unauthorized use or misuse of government motor transportation shall be disallowed in audit.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management consider adopting the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) payroll system, for greater transparency and efficiency and in order to safeguard government funds from the risk of loss or misapplication by disbursing officers. This will also significantly reduce operating costs in the handling and payment of cash to individual payees. It may also as one of the internal control measures that LGU's may adopt to promote good governance at the local level.
observations.managementAction: None

observations.recommendation: 4.5 We reiterated our recommendation that management ensure that the 20% Development Fund be utilized in accordance with the approved budget for priority development programs, projects and activities which shall contribute to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management. Management should stop charging administrative expenditures and recompense the amount of the expenses irregularly charged to the 20% DF. Strict adherence to rules and regulations is enjoined.

5.7 We recommended the management to comply with LBC 103 on the payment of RATA.  Submit the deficiencies noted on the supporting documents of the RATA payments for validation to assess the overpayments made.

6.4 We reiterated our recommendation to management:
Management particularly the HRMO and Accounting Office strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations stated above in the preparation and approval of the payrolls to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance thereof.
In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net take home pay to less than P5,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association shall enjoy second preference and so forth. The other deductions that are not accommodated during the pay period should be paid directly by the employee concerned to the loaning institution.
The HRMO exercise diligence in certifying the monthly net take home pay of employees and provide emergency measures to assuage the situation of those who have been negatively affected by their loan deductions.

7.6 We recommended management to submit the required supporting documents to avoid suspension in audit and strictly adhere to the provisions of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001.
7.7 The observation was well taken by the management.
8.9 We recommended management to submit the required supporting documents to avoid suspension in audit and strictly adhere to the provisions of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001.

9.3 We recommended that management provide the necessary supporting documents of the transactions to avoid suspension in audit and strictly adhere to the provisions of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

10.6 We recommended that management 1.) ensure the strict implementation of its internal controls; 2.) adopt additional measures or activate the use of the biometric machine to avoid manipulation of the time of arrival in the office; and 3.) require the employees to refund any amount paid in excess of the actual time rendered in service or file for proportionate leave of absence, if warranted.

11.7 We reiterated our recommendation that the management consider adopting the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) payroll system, for greater transparency and efficiency in order to safeguard government funds from the risk of loss or misapplication by disbursing officers; reduce operating costs in the handling and payment of cash to individual payees and as one of the internal control measures to promote good governance at the local level.

12.8 We recommended the management to stop the practice of splitting of contracts and strictly adhere to the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 in all the procurements to prevent sanctions for violation of the procurement law.

13.5 We recommended Management to comply with Section 53.5 of RA 9184 and its IRR, Section 4 of Administrative Order No. 17 and paragraph 1.1 of DBM Circular Letter No. 2011-6 in the procurement of Common-Use Supplies from PS-DBM.

14.9 We recommended management to procure common-use supplies from the Procurement Services, post all procurement opportunities in the PhilGEPS bulletin board, stop the practice of procuring supplies by piecemeal and adopt public bidding as the primary mode of procurement in compliance with RA No. 9184.

15.6 We recommended that management ensure that: a) all expenses should be recognized in the period they are incurred; b) unpaid but obligated expenditures at year-end should be recorded as payable; and c) stop paying prior year expenses using current year appropriations. This is to warrant the proper recognition and fair presentation of the financial statements and to avoid depletion of current year funds. We also request the management to give valid justification why the aforementioned disbursements should not be disallowed in audit.
16.7 We recommended management to strictly comply with the provisions of Section 116 and 117 Volume 1 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for Local Government Units (LGU).

17.5 We recommended management to submit the approved POs and their supporting documents to the Office of the Auditor for review within five (5) working days from the issuance thereof as prescribed in COA Circular 2009-001.

18.4 We reiterated our recommendation to management to prepare and submit to the office of the auditor the Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumptions for review as required by COA Circular No. 77-61 which provides the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles.

19.8 We reiterated our recommendation to management to comply with COA Circular No. 77-61 which provides the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles and Section 4 (6) of Presidential Decree No. 1445. All expenses regarding any unauthorized use or misuse of government motor transportation shall be disallowed in audit

20.6 We reiterated our recommendation that management stop the improper charging of expenses, prepare the necessary adjusting entries for proper presentation in financial statements and cease incurring any expenditure without appropriation. Also, we recommended management to submit justification on why the RATA charged against the appropriation for telephone expenses should not be disallowed in audit.

21.5 We recommended that the accounting office maintain property ledger cards for each PPE account, the inventory report prepared by the municipal treasurer’s office should reconcile with accounting records and the necessary adjustments be made to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that management comply with laws and regulations covering the receipt and utilization of funds coming from National Agencies to ensure easier reporting and enhance transparency and accountability in the implementation of the intended programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: Management prepared separate report for the utilization of the Bayanihan Fund since the fund was already exhausted to address programs and projects for COVID-19.

observations.recommendation: We recommended management to replenish the said amount to the Bayanihan Fund and exclusively use it for the allowed COVID-19-related programs, projects, and activities (PPAs) as enumerated in the LBC. observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide depreciation expenses on the road networks presented in the financial statement as provided under Section 262, Volume I of the Government accounting Manual for LGUs. In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net take home pay to less than P5,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association shall enjoy second preference and so forth. observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Prioritize the reconciliation of balances between the accounting records and RPCPPE. Prepare adjusting entries, if any, to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts. 
Maintain a detailed PPE Ledger Card for each class of Property, Plant and Equipment that supports the balances presented in the FS. The schedule should enable the user to trace for the acquisition date and cost of an individual PPE to facilitate depreciation computation.
observations.managementAction: The management has complied to the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following to the Management:

a. The Municipal Engineer shall provide the OIC- Municipal Accountant with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

b. The LGU shall maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and shall keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components.

c. The OIC-Municipal Accountant shall prepare the Journal Voucher to record the inventory of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card.  He shall keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network, and ensure that 100% of the value of road network of the LGU shall be booked-up in 2019 consistent with COA Circular No. 2015-008. Full disclosure on road networks should be made in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the LGU
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Engineer submitted the inventory of Local Road Networks and Road Maps within the area of jurisdiction of the local government unit.
The Acting Municipal Accountant 
prepared the necessary journal entries.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Local Chief Executive strictly comply with the Local Government Code of 1991 by appointing soonest a permanent Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to further strengthen the internal control unit/budget system of the Local Government Unit.

We recommended the following:
Management particularly the HRMO and Accounting Office strictly comply with the existing rules and regulations stated above in the preparation and approval of the payrolls to avoid sanctions that may be imposed for non-compliance thereof.
In the event that the total authorized deductions reduce the net take home pay to less than P5,000.00, the authorized deductions for BIR, GSIS, HDMF and PHIC shall enjoy first preference, those deductions for mutual benefits association, thrift banks and savings and loan association shall enjoy second preference and so forth. The other deductions that are not accommodated during the pay period should be paid directly by the employee concerned to the loaning institution.
The HRMO exercise diligence in certifying the monthly net take home pay of employees and provide emergency measures to assuage the situation of those who have been negatively affected by their loan deductions.

We recommended the management to ensure completeness of supporting documents to avoid suspension in audit and strictly adhere to the provisions of Presidential Decree No. 1445, COA Circular No. 2012-001 and, Rule XVII, Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292.

We recommended that Management:
Prioritize the reconciliation of balances between the accounting records and RPCPPE. Prepare adjusting entries, if any, to reflect the correct balance of the affected accounts.
Maintain a detailed PPE Ledger Card for each class of Property, Plant and Equipment that supports the balances presented in the FS. The schedule should enable the user to trace for the acquisition date and cost of an individual PPE to facilitate depreciation computation.

We recommended the following to the Management:
a.The Municipal Engineer shall provide the OIC- Municipal Accountant with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
b.	The LGU shall maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and shall keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components.
c.	The OIC-Municipal Accountant shall prepare the Journal Voucher to record the inventory of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card.  He shall keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network, and ensure that 100% of the value of road network of the LGU shall be booked-up in 2019 consistent with COA Circular No. 2015-008. Full disclosure on road networks should be made in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the LGU.
We recommended the OIC-Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting journal entries and strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended to management that the outstanding balances of cash advances be deducted from salaries, if warranted. Strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004 is enjoined.

7.3 We commended management for the LDRRMO accomplishments and awards with a recommendation to comply with the staffing requirements as provided under RA 10121.

8.3 We recommended that management require the concerned employees and/or officials to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period. No cash advance shall be granted unless the outstanding balances is liquidated by them. Disbursements contrary to rules and regulations will be disallowed in audit, if warranted.

9.4 We commended the management for its effort in implementing solid waste management programs with a recommendation to continue encouraging the participation of every constituent in doing its share in environmental protection and maintenance.
11.2 We recommended that management exert more efforts and exhaust all possible means to collect the above audit disallowances.

13.6 We recommended that management provide the necessary supporting documents of the transactions to avoid suspension in audit and strictly adhere to the provisions of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

14.6 We recommended that management always ensure that claims for travelling expenses and per diems are in compliance to Executive Order No. 298 to avoid incurrence of irregular, unnecessary, excessive, extravagant and unconscionable expenditures which can be disallowed in audit.

15.5 We recommended that management strictly adhere to the provisions of R.A. 9184 on the procurement of goods to obtain the most advantageous price for the government. Canvass from suppliers of known qualifications should always be observed to establish that the best terms for the procurement and best quality of the items were indeed obtained. Overpricing, if proven, will be disallowed in audit.

16.4  We reiterated our recommendation that  the Local Chief Executive strictly comply with the Local Government Code of 1991 by appointing soonest a permanent Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to further strengthen the internal control unit/budget system of the Local Government Unit.

We recommend that management cancel the issued stale checks and make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct status of the cash in bank account for proper presentation in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The management has already made an adjusting entry for the stale checks in the books.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant issue final demand letters to all active officials and employees with outstanding balances and suspend their salaries, if warranted. Strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004 is enjoined.
observations.managementAction:
The management already issued a demand letter informing the concerned municipal officials and employees for the settlement of their cash advances. 
Request for write off has already been requested for dormant accounts.
Some of the employees concerned monetized their earned leaves for them to settle their cash advances.
observations.reason: Reiterated as finding no. 2 of this report. Some accountable officers with outstanding cash advances are no longer connected to the agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management comply with the provisions of PPSAS 12 relative to the recording of inventories.
observations.managementAction: Inventory account was set up for some procured supplies at year-end.
observations.reason: Reiterated as finding no. 3 of this report. Some payments for supplies were still charged to expense account.

observations.recommendation: We reiterate the previous year audit recommendations that management conduct actual inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and the corresponding report be prepared and submitted to comply with COA Circular No. 92-386. The inventory report prepared by the GSO should reconcile with accounting records and necessary adjustments should be made to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The office of the Municipal Treasurer in partnership with the office of the Acting Municipal Accountant conducted the inventory of PPE and the reconciliation thereof.
observations.reason: Reiterated as finding no. 4 of this report. The office of the Municipal Accountant failed to maintain property ledger cards.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant identify the details of the recorded land account and ascertain all acquisitions that were not yet transferred to the LGU and for management to expedite the titling of all land acquired by the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The OIC- Municipal Accountant tried to get the list of all lots owned by the Local Government from the Municipal Assessor to identify the details and amount of the said properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer make strategies, conduct massive tax information campaign and exert efforts to ensure collection of all realty taxes.  Demand letters should be sent regularly to the delinquent owners, and if warranted, their names including the amount of their realty tax delinquencies should be posted in public places so that the public may know who they are.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer conducted massive tax information campaign and issued a payment notice to the taxpayers about their real property tax delinquencies.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management comply with the provisions of RA 10121 on the staffing requirements as well as the observance of the required volume of stockpile to be maintained at the LDRRMO.
observations.managementAction: The local Government has appointed a salary grade 15 in Disaster Risk Reduction Staff as a requirement and two staff and other augmentation through hiring of casual personnel.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the management exert more efforts and exhaust all possible means to collect the above audit disallowances.
observations.managementAction: Requested for write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management issue policy guidelines in the hiring of job order/contractual employees in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 and Sections 209 and 211(b), Volume I of the GAAM and to strictly implement the same.
observations.managementAction: A resolution was made as a basis in hiring job orders/casuals.

observations.recommendation: We commend the management for their initiate to request for authority to write-off from dormant receivable accounts from COA with the recommendation that the Acting Municipal Accountant should prepare and submit the required supporting documents needed for the write-off as required under COA Circular 2016-005 to facilitate the disposition thereof.
observations.managementAction: Documents were submitted to COA and some accounts were already approved for write-off.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management consider adopting the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) payroll system, for greater transparency and efficiency and in order to safeguard government funds from the risk of loss or misapplication by disbursing officers. This will also significantly reduce operating costs in the handling and payment of cash to individual payees.
observations.managementAction: The ATM-type payroll system was not adopted by the LGU.

observations.recommendation: 5 We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and the Acting LDRRM Officer strictly follow the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No.  2012-002 dated September12, 2012 on the reporting and accounting of LDRRM Funds.
observations.managementAction: The monthly report of LDRRM utilization was not submitted for the year 2017.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management stop the practice of procuring regular office supplies by department/office, instead require the BAC Secretariat to consolidate all needed supplies of departments/offices of the municipality based on their individual Project Procurement Plan (PPMP) to the Annual Procurement Plan (APP). Based on the APP, the BAC should adopt bulk purchases through public bidding to obtain the price most advantageous to the municipality pursuant to RA 9184 and ITS Revised IRR in order to promote transparency, efficiency and economy in the procurement of the office supplies.
observations.managementAction: Procurement of regular office supplies remain on a per office basis.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that  the Local Chief Executive strictly comply with the Local Government Code of 1991 by appointing soonest a permanent Municipal Accountant and Budget Officer to further strengthen the internal control unit/budget system of the Local Government Unit.

We commend the management for the fast implementation of projects funded by the different national government agencies with a recommendation to coordinate with the national government agency concerned for the possible alternative solutions to problems encountered during the implementation.

We recommend that management cancel the issued stale checks and make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct status of the cash in bank account for proper presentation in the financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant issue final demand letters to all active officials and employees with outstanding balances and suspend their salaries, if warranted. Strict adherence to the provisions of COA Circular Nos. 97-002 and 2012-004 is enjoined.
We recommended that management comply with the provisions of PPSAS 12 relative to the recording of inventories.

We reiterate the previous year audit recommendations that management  conduct actual inventory of all properties owned by the LGU and the corresponding report be prepared and submitted to comply with COA Circular No. 92-386. The inventory report prepared by the GSO should reconcile with accounting records and necessary adjustments should be made to present a reliable balance in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant identify the details of the recorded land account and ascertain all acquisitions that were not yet transferred to the LGU and for management to expedite the titling of all land acquired by the municipality.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer make strategies, conduct massive tax information campaign and exert efforts to ensure collection of all realty taxes.  Demand letters should be sent regularly to the delinquent owners, and if warranted, their names including the amount of their realty tax delinquencies should be posted in public places so that the public may know who they are.

We recommend that the Management ensure that the 20% Development Fund of the LGU be allotted and be used for priority development programs, projects and activities as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.
We recommend that management comply with the provisions of RA 10121 on the staffing requirements as well as the observance of the required volume of stockpile to be maintained at the LDRRMO.
We recommend that the management exert more efforts and exhaust all possible means to collect the above audit disallowances.

We recommend that management issue policy guidelines in the hiring of contractual/job order employees in accordance with Section 2, Rule XI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive Order No. 292 and Sections 209 and 211(b), Volume I of the GAAM and to strictly implement the same.

Wecommend the management for their initiate to request for authority to write-off from dormant receivable accounts from COA with the recommendation that the Acting Municipal Accountant should prepare and submit the required supporting documents needed for the write-off as required under COA Circular 2016-005 to facilitate the disposition thereof.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) implement all downloaded funds in accordance with the provisions of Republic Act 9184 otherwise known as the Government Procurement Act and that all payments for the procurement of goods and services be made by check directly to suppliers, dealers or creditors.
observations.managementAction: Most payments of goods and services were made by check directly paid to supplier but there remain some procurements which were done thru cash advance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management conduct trainings/seminars that will enhance the capability and skills of the Barangay Health Workers in providing basic services about health and the use of health equipment. The head of the Barangay Health Office is encouraged to make proper representation with the Municipal Government, as conditioned by necessity, for the improvement of the BHU facilities and to address concerns of patients and health workers.
observations.managementAction: Orientation seminar on the use of health equipment was conducted to all barangay health workers.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management publish tax declaration numbers of delinquent taxpayers so that they will be informed of their obligations. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to review the list of delinquent taxpayers because from the confirmation made, some has already settled their accounts. The ultimate goal of improving the collection efficiency and effectiveness ratio of RPT is not remote with the collective efforts of the legislative council by supporting the local revenue code, the office of the Municipal Assessor by undertaking timely revisions and simultaneous cleansing of the RPTAS database and the office of the Municipal Treasurer by strengthening the force of law in availing itself of other more effective and powerful remedies so provided in enforcing collection of delinquent accounts such as administrative action thru levy on real property or by judicial action.

We reiterated our recommendation that officials and employees of the municipal government with outstanding cash advance be directed to immediately liquidate/settle their cash advances to avoid the possibility of being charged due to misuse of government funds. If warranted, withhold the salaries of those who fail or refuse to comply with the regulations. Henceforth, monitor closely all outstanding cash advances and demand immediately the liquidations. Management should strictly enforce the time limits in the liquidation of cash advances.
observations.managementAction: Demand letters were issued to personnel and employees with outstanding balances and the cash advance decreased due to the submission of liquidation reports.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management strictly monitor and require the collectors to regularly remit intact all their collections to avoid misapplication of government funds and to reflect all income collected during the year.

We recommended that management evaluate the amount of bond coverage and whenever necessary, increase their coverage with the Fidelity Bond of the BT especially the Municipal Treasurer/Assistant Municipal Treasurer  since they are handling large sums of money turned over to them by the collecting officers pending deposit to the authorized depository bank.
observations.managementAction: The amount of bonds was increased during their renewal for the year 2017.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendation that the management strictly utilize the 20% Development fund for the purpose the fund was created. Strictly adhere to the provisions of Joint DBM, DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 11, 2011.
observations.managementAction: Various expenses were still charged in the 20% DF even if they were not among those allowed under the DBM-DILG Joint MC.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management comply with the provisions of the IRR of RA 10121 through the provision in the annual  budget  salaries of  a full pledge LDRRMO and  one (1)  LDRRM Assistant.
observations.managementAction: The required number of staff for the LDRRM Office was not yet fully complied with.

observations.recommendation: 11.4 We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant record in the books of the municipality the cost of Public Infrastructure projects as well as cost of completed and constructed projects through trust funds.

We recommended that the management furnish the approved/perfected infrastructure contracts and their supporting documents to the Audit Team for review within five (5) working days from the execution of the contract as prescribed in COA Circular 2009 001 dated February 12, 2009 to enable the Audit Team to conduct a timely auditorial review and evaluation of contracts and communicate to the agency the results thereof.

We reiterated our recommendation that management :1)Identify and allocate an area to serve as a central storage for supplies to be carried in stock; 2)In the meantime that no central storeroom is available, require all heads of departments to designate a person within their department to act as supplies custodian who will account for all receipts and issuances using the NGAS forms prescribed for Local Government Units; 3)Require the Municipal Treasurer to maintain supplies ledger cards; 4)Require the Accounting Office to observe perpetual inventory system in the evaluation of inventories; 5)Require the Inventory Team to conduct periodic inventory of supplies and render a report especially at year end and set up inventory account; 6)  Ensure that the year-end balance of the inventory accounts corresponds to the value of supplies items, actually on hand at year end; and 7)Establish control procedures to regulate the issuances of supplies to actual requirements of the end users within a month.
observations.managementAction: No central storage can be allocated due to the limited space of the municipality, however, logbooks were maintained for the requisition of supplies of each office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the foregoing misstatements in the financial statements of the municipality. We also recommended that the management stop charging assistance to various constituents from the 20% Development Fund because it was not intended for donations instead in uplifting the lives of its constituents through various programs/projects.
observations.managementAction: The AICS granted in CY 2017 were charged in CY 2017 were charged in the GF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Accounting Office book- up the cost of one unit tractor as part of the Property, Plant and Equipment and the corresponding Loans Payable account in the books of the local government unit.  Consistent thereto, properly include in the annual budget provisions for amortization as per Memorandum of Agreement.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary documents needed for the write-off as required by COA Circular 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 and submit the request to COA for write-off of long outstanding receivables particularly those from retired or deceased employees.
observations.managementAction: Request for authority to write-off was submitted to COA.
